source: REAL headless FCP PAEColorize (FCT_COLORIZE_WS), gray endpoints (bk,wt) x luma ramp -> center RGB
goal: decode the intermediate-endpoint luma-remap coupling (the documented Colorize/Tint gap)

confirmed_exact.bk=0 case: out_code = in_code * s2l(wt)  (white endpoint = a code-space MULTIPLY by its LINEAR value; maxerr 0.35 lvl for wt=0.5 and wt=1.0)

best_partial_model_Hyp_G.formula: out_code = 255*( (in/255)*s2l(wt) + (1 - in/255)*s2l(bk) )  [code-space linear blend, endpoints as LINEAR values]
best_partial_model_Hyp_G.fit: EXACT for bk=0 (maxerr 0.22); 13.4 lvl max error for bk!=0 (mid-tone curvature unresolved)
best_partial_model_Hyp_G.meaning: white contributes in*L(wt), black contributes (1-in)*L(bk), both in code space with linear-valued endpoints; the residual is a mid-tone curvature coupling still to decode

verified_regime: Remap Black To=(0,0,0) + Remap White To in {any primary, white, gray}; intermediate-VALUE black endpoints (bk>0) hit the mid-tone coupling. Shipping Colorize users author bk=0 so the shipped path is verified.

interp_param_finding.residual_shape: Hyp G residual is EXACTLY 0 at Y=0 and Y=255, bulging +13 lvl in the mid-tones (peak ~Y=64), IDENTICAL shape for both (0.2,0.8) and (0.3,0.7) pairs -> the bulge is an input-luma interp-param curve, not an endpoint tint error.
interp_param_finding.recovered_t: solving out=255*(t*L(wt)+(1-t)*L(bk)) for t gives t(Y) monotonic 0->1, slightly ABOVE Y/255 (t=0.33 vs 0.25 @Y=64), but t DIFFERS across pairs for bk>0 (Y=64: 0.343/0.391/0.253 for (0.2,0.8)/(0.3,0.7)/(0,0.5)) -> the interp param COUPLES to the endpoint pair when bk>0.
interp_param_finding.clean_boundary: bk=0 -> t is exactly the code-space multiply (out=in*L(wt)); bk>0 -> the black (1-t) term couples the interp param. Needs a denser (bk,wt) grid to close. Shipping authors bk=0 (verified/shipped path clean).

dense_grid_wt1.note: Dense bk sweep at wt=1.0 (code-space lerp bk_code->255, recovered t(Y,bk)). For bk=0, t(Y)=Y/255 EXACTLY (pure code-space identity). For bk>0, t goes NEGATIVE at low Y (output DROPS BELOW the black endpoint) and t@Y128 falls 0.502->0.435 as bk 0->0.4 — so Colorize is NOT a bounded endpoint lerp; the black endpoint couples the whole curve non-linearly. All tested linear-space models (lerp/multiply/additive with s2l endpoints) fail for bk>0 (23-68 lvl). The coupling is likely a hue/per-channel-space interp that doesn't separate on grays.
dense_grid_wt1.conclusion: bk=0 fully decoded (identity/multiply, shipped path). bk>0 coupling needs the HgcColorize shader disasm or a per-channel (non-gray) probe — not closeable with gray-endpoint linear-space fits.
